items.label: Select the target track and full research paper type
Paste and verify the title from the metadata packet
Paste and verify the abstract from the metadata packet

prefill.summary: Large language models (LLMs) are increasingly used as scoring and representation engines for recommendation, but high ranking scores do not by themselves tell us when a recommendation decision is reliable. We study this problem under a controlled same-candidate protocol: every method ranks the same 101 candidates for each user, uses the same Qwen3-8B backbone when an LLM is required, and is evaluated with the same importer, metrics, provenance checks, and paired tests. We propose C-CRP, a task-grounded calibrated candidate relevance posterior that decomposes uncertainty into boundary ambiguity, calibration gap, evidence support, and counterevidence, and defines a validation-controlled ranking family whose zero-risk special case is explicitly allowed. Across Sports, Toys, Home, and Tools, C-CRP ranks first against eight official-code-level LLM-based recommendation baselines on HR@5/@10/@20, NDCG@5/@10/@20, and MRR. All 56 per-domain paired C-CRP-vs-baseline tests are positive and Holm-significant in each domain. We also provide paper-critical diagnostic evidence: event-level uncertainty stratifies ranking reliability in all four domains, component ablations reveal that several uncertainty terms are weak or redundant rather than uniformly necessary, and validation-selected hyperparameters are stable within the pre-registered tolerance on NDCG@10. The result is a scoped claim: the C-CRP ranking line improves controlled same-candidate ranking, while its uncertainty signal stratifies reliability; it is not a full-catalog recommender SOTA claim.

prefill.summary: LLM-based recommendation, uncertainty estimation, calibration, candidate reranking, same-candidate evaluation
Recommender systems, Evaluation and reproducibility, Large language models, Uncertainty and calibration

items.label: Upload source package if required by the submission system
Confirm anonymous PDF/source shell in the submission system
Enter authors, affiliations, and order inside the submission system
Complete conflict-of-interest fields inside the submission system
Complete reviewer suggestions/exclusions only inside the submission system if requested
Complete submission-system declarations, ethics, artifact, and policy checkboxes
Confirm external proceedings metadata blockers are closed
Review final submission preview and submit

next_actions: Use this checklist while filling the submission system; do not copy private author/COI/reviewer data into the repository.
Rerun the external proceedings metadata audit immediately before final submission.
Rerun submission package, metadata packet, and this checklist after any paper/source/BibTeX change.
Keep final_submission_ready=false until manual submission-system items and external metadata blockers are all closed.
remaining_blockers: ProMax final ACM page range and ACM/Crossref registry visibility must be rechecked immediately before submission.
Final manual submission-system metadata/format checklist is not closed.
